name: Baked Apple Zapata
recipeIngredient: 3 Granny Smith apples, peeled and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Use your favorite recipe for flour tortillas or make dough from the Tortilla mix package.
2. Divide the dough into 4, 2-inch balls. Roll each ball into a 6-inch tortilla.
3. Dust with flour and cover with plastic wrap; set aside.
4. In a saucepan, place apples in enough water to cover. Add lemon juice.
5. Cook over high heat until boiling for 5 minutes.
6. Add sugar, ground cloves, ground cinnamon, and chopped walnuts. Continue cooking over low heat until slightly thickened.
7. Dissolve cornstarch with water. Stir into the mixture until smooth.
8. Remove from heat. Lay out tortillas; remove excess flour.
9. Spoon 2 tablespoons of the apple mixture down the middle of each tortilla.
10. Fold one side of the tortilla over the mixture, then fold the remaining side over.
11. Fold in the ends and seal by moistening the edges with water.
12. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F until lightly browned.
13. Remove from heat and sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar. Serve with whipped cream or Tequila Sabayon sauce (made with tequila, egg yolks, and sugar).
